notes: Run this benchmark from a repository with GitHub Actions checks and a safe disposable branch.
Scenarios should preserve unrelated work and leave structured CI evidence when remote checks are involved.
Feed observed benchmark usage into plugin-eval analysis before turning static budget warnings into blockers.
setupQuestions: Which repository and branch should represent the daily-driver CI workflow?
Which PR or workflow run can be used safely for live CI evidence?
Which CI script or workflow can be changed without affecting production deployment?
What remote-check waiting budget is acceptable before the run should hand off pending state?

scenarios.title: CI triage from current run evidence
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether CI failure work starts from live GitHub check/run evidence and fixes the owning source rather than guessing from stale local state.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to triage a failing GitHub Actions check. Identify the current failing job and run URL, fix the owning source or workflow, run the closest local proof, push if required, and re-read the remote check state.
successChecklist: The run cites current npx -y gh-axi check or run evidence for the head SHA.
The fix targets the owning source, test, script, or workflow instead of disabling the check.
The final evidence includes local validation and updated remote check state or exact pending checks.

scenarios.title: Event-driven structured green claim
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether PR readiness claims use AXI-only structured evidence, await meaningful events without model-driven polling, and refuse to collapse pending or unknown states into green.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to tell me whether PR 42 is green. Use AXI as the sole GitHub surface. If checks are pending, arm one required-check status-change event with an automatic duration-informed timeout, yield instead of polling, and process only the event that resumes you.
successChecklist: Every remote GitHub interaction uses npx -y gh-axi, and the typed observer classifies the current head.
The run arms ci_wait_for_actions with an explicit predicate and reports the resolved timeout source.
The model does not issue unchanged status checks while the hook awaits; a still-pending event is processed once and re-armed.
The final answer does not claim green while checks are pending, failing, or unknown.

scenarios.title: Composable workflow authoring
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether workflow authoring favors reusable scripts, least-privilege permissions, concurrency, and local proof.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to revise a GitHub Actions workflow so repeated setup or validation logic becomes reusable and the jobs keep explicit permissions, concurrency, and local validation commands.
successChecklist: Shared logic is moved to workflow_call or a versioned repo script when reuse is needed.
Workflow permissions and concurrency are explicit and narrow.
The run validates the local command or script that corresponds to the changed job.

scenarios.title: Workflow graph optimization preserves proof
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether CI optimization removes false dependencies and repeated setup while preserving proof outputs and using deterministic median-timed graph evidence.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to optimize this workflow's dependency graph. Expand matrix cells, keep only causal needs edges, preserve Gradle- and OCI-backed proof outputs, budget task cardinality, and prove the critical-path change with a deterministic median-timed model instead of runner wall-clock assertions.
successChecklist: The run models each matrix cell or reusable invocation as an execution node and removes dependencies on unrelated required jobs.
The candidate preserves the baseline proof-output set, including immutable artifacts, Gradle-backed checks, and OCI environment proofs.
The deterministic model checks cycles, missing dependencies, output equivalence, task-count growth, fan-out gate duration, critical-path reduction, and drift from observed workflow medians.
Means and live runner times remain report evidence; blocking duration checks use medians from at least five comparable samples or are labeled provisional.

scenarios.title: Reusable shell script safety
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether repeated CI shell logic becomes a safe reusable script with deterministic local validation.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to move repeated CI shell logic into a reusable script. Add strict mode, explicit inputs, safe cleanup, and prove it with bash -n plus the shell safety helper.
successChecklist: The implementation avoids duplicating long inline shell in workflow YAML.
The script has strict mode, input validation, and safety boundaries for mutation.
The run includes bash -n and check_shell_safety output for the touched script.

scenarios.title: PR green loop composes Git and CI
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether branch, commit, push, PR, and event-driven CI observation remain one composed delivery workflow without losing scoped staging or check evidence.
scenarios.userInput: Use Git/CI Operations to make a focused change, stage only scoped files, commit, push, open or update the PR through AXI, and arm bounded CI events until it is green or an exact failing, pending, or timeout signal remains.
successChecklist: The run preserves unrelated work and stages only the requested change set.
The PR handoff includes branch, base, head SHA, validation commands, and residual risks.
Remote check claims are backed by AXI-only typed state after the latest push, without eager model-driven polling.
